title: 5 Self Care Routines to Heal your Body and Mind #shorts #diabetes
description:
5 self-care routines to heal for people with diabetes:

Workout first thing in the morning to get glucose under control: I find that working out first thing in the morning without insulin works the best to not only control my blood glucose throughout the day but also prevents any lows

Mindfulness practice & breathing exercise: I find meditation and or breathing exercises like yoga help tremendously with mental health

Counting carbs precisely: I like to think of counting carbs as self care because it keeps your blood glucose in control after meals. Doing so will prevent diabetes burnouts

Treat your cuts and burns with @Lavior Diabetic first aid gel: It is important to take care of wounds living with diabetes. First aid gel is formulated to prevent infection and relieves pain and itch. It gives relief and promotes healing of cuts, scrapes, burns, sunburns.

Heal your dark marks from diabetes devices with Lavior Diabetic wound dressing: It creates optimal environment for wound healing and promotes skin regeneration. I like to apply the wound dressing to the red marks right after site change.

#skincare #diabetes #type1diabetes #typ2diabetes #diabeteseducation #diabetesawareness

AVENA Y DIABETES 🥣🛑 5 ERRORES frecuentes al consumir AVENA
description:
Te cuento como es que la avena te puede ayudar a con la diabetes y 5 errores comunes que hacen que la avena suba tu glucosa. puedes ir directamente:
0:42 Beneficios de la canela
1:24 La avena ayuda a regular los niveles de glucosa
3:37 Errores al comer la avena
3:51 No conocer las porciones
4:44 No comprar avena integral
5:52 Comer avena instantánea
7:16 Creer que todo lo que tiene avena es saludable
8:17 Comer la avena sola

INSTAGRAM - https://www.instagram.com/diabesmart/

💊 10% de DESCUENTO en tu primer compra de medicamentos usando nuestro código 👉 DISMDL24 https://bit.ly/4ccAWiz (solo méxico)

___

La avena es rica en fibra, especialmente en fibra soluble, que ayuda a regular el azúcar en sangre y a mejorar la digestión. La fibra soluble puede ralentizar la absorción de glucosa, lo que puede ser beneficioso para las personas con diabetes.

El índice glucémico es una medida de cuán rápido un alimento aumenta el nivel de glucosa en sangre. Los alimentos con un IG bajo se digieren y absorben más lentamente, lo que puede ayudar a controlar los niveles de azúcar en sangre. La avena integral tiene un IG moderado, por lo que puede ser una opción adecuada para las personas con diabetes, siempre que se consuma con moderación y se sigan algunas pautas.

Betaglucanos y su impacto en la salud

Los betaglucanos son un tipo de fibra soluble presente en la avena, que se ha relacionado con beneficios para la salud cardiovascular y el control de la glucosa en sangre. Estudios científicos han demostrado que los betaglucanos pueden ayudar a reducir el colesterol LDL (colesterol malo) y a mejorar la sensibilidad a la insulina, lo que podría ser beneficioso para las personas con diabetes.

Recomendaciones para consumir avena en caso de diabetes

Evitar la avena instantánea: La avena instantánea de Quaker, por ejemplo, suele tener menos fibra que la avena integral. Opta por avena integral, que es menos procesada y contiene más fibra.

Asegurarse de que sea integral: La avena integral debe tener un aspecto más oscuro y menos uniforme que la avena refinada.

Acompañar con grasas saludables o proteínas: Combinar la avena con grasas saludables (como nueces o almendras) o proteínas (como yogurt griego) puede ayudar a reducir su índice glucémico y a mantener estables los niveles de azúcar en sangre.

Evitar la fruta cocida: La fruta cocida puede aumentar el IG de la avena, por lo que es mejor evitarla.

Elegir versiones sin azúcar: Algunas marcas de avena ofrecen opciones sin azúcar. Asegúrate de elegir estas opciones para evitar un consumo excesivo de azúcares.

Combinar con ingredientes adecuados: Mezcla la avena con leche vegetal, nueces, almendras, frutos rojos o fresas para obtener un desayuno equilibrado y nutritivo. También puedes combinarla con chía y dejarla remojando para potenciar sus beneficios.

Evitar la avena refinada: La avena también puede estar refinada, lo que disminuye su contenido de fibra y aumenta su índice glucémico. Opta por la avena integral siempre que sea posible.

Tener cuidado al cocinar la avena: Cocinar la avena puede elevar su índice glucémico. Considera remojarla en agua o yogurt griego en lugar de cocinarla.

Consumir avena con moderación: A pesar de sus beneficios, la avena sigue siendo una fuente de carbohidratos. Si tienes diabetes, es importante consumirla con moderación y ajustar su ingesta según tus necesidades nutricionales y los consejos de tu médico o dietista.

Evitar ciertos tipos de avena: Algunas variedades de avena, como la granola, suelen contener azúcares añadidos, grasas y frutos secos en exceso. Estos ingredientes pueden aumentar su índice glucémico, por lo que es mejor evitarlos.

Ser creativo con recetas saludables: Puedes preparar hot cakes de avena con huevo, o incluso un pastel de zanahoria saludable usando avena integral. Esto te permitirá disfrutar de la avena de diversas maneras sin comprometer tu salud.

title: How To Reverse Diabetes Naturally ?
description:
Want to know how to reverse diabetes naturally? Here are 3 powerful strategies to manage blood sugar levels effectively.

 1. Food Sequencing: Eat your veggies first, followed by protein and fats, and save carbs for last. This helps control blood sugar spikes by slowing down carb absorption, which keeps insulin levels steady.
 2. Exercise and Weight Training: Regular exercise, especially weight training, is key to improving insulin sensitivity. By building muscle, you help your body use glucose more effectively and reduce blood sugar levels naturally.
 3. Use a CGM (Continuous Glucose Monitor): A CGM patch helps you track your blood sugar in real-time, so you know exactly which foods trigger spikes and which keep you stable. This personalized insight can be a game-changer in managing diabetes.

Watch until the end to learn how these simple habits can transform your health journey!

title: CHAOTIC OMNIPOD CHANGE 😅💉 #t1d #typeonediabetes #diabetes #omnipod #omnipoddash #youtubeshorts
Freeze your rice to prevent glucose spike #diabetes
description:
This is HOW I freeze my rice. First off, I never eat just white rice, it always has a minimum of quinoa and oatmeal mixed in. Sometimes I’ll add chia seeds but purple rice is one of my favorites! 

When you cook rice the heat makes the starch molecules absorb water and swell which makes the rice soft and easy to digest. But when you cool and freeze the rice, starch molecules reorganize into a crystalline structure which turns into resistance starch. 

Honestly, it’s kind of therapeutic to freeze and organize my blocks of rice into my freezer bag. Who needs instant microwave rice when you can get a hot bowl of rice in a little over a minute, straight out of your freezer, instead of your rice cooker?

Freezer silicone trays: https://amzn.to/4fkQnX3

title: ENT Symptoms of Diabetes || Dr. Deepthi Kareti
description:
How does diabetes affect the ears?
Over time, high blood sugar levels can damage small blood vessels and nerves in the inner ear. Low blood sugar over time can damage how nerve signals travel from the inner ear to your brain. Both types of nerve damage can lead to hearing loss.

What is the most common ear infection in diabetics?
Malignant otitis externa and rhinocerebral mucormycosis are two head-and-neck infections seen almost exclusively in patients with diabetes.

How common is hearing loss in diabetes?
Audiogram was analyzed for hearing loss &classified by WHO criteria found that 72 % individuals with diabetes were noted to have mild SNHL ,20 % with moderate SNHL,3% moderate severe SNHL, 4 % severe ,1% with profound hearing loss .

Can diabetes cause itchy ears?
Diabetes. Certain systemic disorders, such as diabetes, make a person more prone to ear infections and itchiness. This is because diabetes makes earwax less acidic, which makes the skin in the ears more vulnerable to fungal and bacterial infections.

Can diabetics use ear drops?
Make sure you tell your doctor if you have any other medical problems, especially: Type 2 diabetes mellitus or. Epilepsy—Using otic corticosteroids may worsen this condition.

title: Is papaya or papita safe for diabetes patients?
description:
Is Papaya (Papita) Safe for Diabetes Patients?
✅ Yes, papaya is safe for diabetics when consumed in moderation. It has moderate carbohydrates and a low glycemic index (GI) of around 60, meaning it won’t spike blood sugar drastically. However, portion control is key!

📌 Why Is Papaya Good for Diabetics?
🍈 Moderate Carbohydrates – A 100g serving of papaya contains 11g of carbs, which is lower than many high-GI fruits like bananas or mangoes.
🌿 Rich in Fiber – Papaya contains 2.5g of fiber per 100g, which helps slow down sugar absorption and prevents sudden glucose spikes.
🛡️ Antioxidants & Vitamins – It is packed with vitamin C, A, and flavonoids, which support immune function and reduce inflammation.

⚠️ How Much Papaya Can a Diabetic Eat?
🔹 Stick to 100-150g per serving (about a small bowl or 3-4 medium slices).
🔹 Avoid eating papaya with other high-carb foods to prevent a blood sugar spike.
🔹 Best time to eat? As a mid-morning snack or post-meal dessert with protein/fat to slow down sugar absorption.

🥇 Is Guava Better Than Papaya for Diabetics?
✅ Yes! Guava is a better choice because:
✔️ Lower glycemic index (~24-40) than papaya
✔️ Higher fiber (5.4g per 100g), making it even better for blood sugar control
✔️ More vitamin C, which helps in reducing oxidative stress in diabetics

If you are choosing between papaya and guava, guava is the better option for diabetics due to its higher fiber and lower sugar content.

🛑 Avoid Papaya If:
🚫 You consume too much fruit in one sitting (excess carbs = blood sugar spikes)
🚫 You have poorly controlled diabetes or insulin resistance
🚫 You eat it along with high-GI fruits like mango, banana, or dates

✅ Final Verdict: Should Diabetics Eat Papaya?
🔹 Yes, in moderation! Papaya is a good fruit choice but should be eaten in safe limits.
🔹 Guava is a better alternative due to its higher fiber content and lower glycemic impact.
🔹 Always monitor blood sugar levels after consuming fruit to see how your body responds.

💬 Which fruit do you prefer—papaya or guava? Let us know in the comments!

Methi Seeds for Diabetes in Telugu || Dr. Deepthi Kareti
description:
What are the benefits of fenugreek seeds?
Fenugreek may aid metabolic conditions, such as diabetes ( 8 ). In one study, people with type 2 diabetes who took 5 g of fenugreek seed powder twice daily for 2 months experienced a reduction in fasting blood sugar levels, belly fat, body mass index, and hemoglobin A1c, a marker of long-term blood sugar control ( 9 ).

Can I use fenugreek seeds daily?
Dosing. Fenugreek seed powder has most often been used by adults in doses of 5-10 grams by mouth daily for up to 3 years. Fenugreek seed extract has most often been used in doses of 0.6-1.2 grams by mouth daily.

Does fenugreek have side effects?
Potential side effects of fenugreek include diarrhea, nausea, and other digestive tract symptoms and rarely, dizziness and headaches. Large doses may cause a harmful drop in blood sugar. Fenugreek can cause allergic reactions in some people.

Can fenugreek reduce belly fat?
Methi or fenugreek has Galactomannan, a component that helps in curbing appetite and improving metabolism. Methi also helps in digestion and promotes belly fat loss. You can add methi to stir-fries, mathris, parathas and a range of delicious dishes.

title: Type1 and Type 2 Diabetes - Know the difference |Dr.Tulip | JP Nagar
description:
Glucose, insulin, the pancreas, and beta cells all play crucial roles in the regulation of blood sugar levels in the body. In a normal person, the process of maintaining proper blood sugar levels is a well-coordinated dance between these components. When we eat, our digestive system breaks down carbohydrates into glucose, which is then absorbed into the bloodstream. As blood sugar levels rise, the pancreas is triggered to release insulin, a hormone that acts as a key to unlock cells, allowing them to take in the glucose and use it for energy production. This process helps to keep blood sugar levels within a healthy range.

In type 1 diabetes, the immune system mistakenly attacks and destroys the beta cells in the pancreas, which are responsible for producing insulin. As a result, the body is unable to produce enough insulin to regulate blood sugar effectively. Without insulin, glucose builds up in the bloodstream, leading to high blood sugar levels. People with type 1 diabetes require insulin therapy to manage their condition, typically through injections or an insulin pump. They need to closely monitor their blood sugar levels and calculate the appropriate insulin doses to keep their blood sugar in check. This constant management is vital since uncontrolled high blood sugar levels can lead to serious health complications over time.

In type 2 diabetes, the body becomes resistant to the effects of insulin, and the pancreas may not be able to produce enough insulin to compensate for this resistance. This results in elevated blood sugar levels. Risk factors for type 2 diabetes include obesity, a sedentary lifestyle, and genetic predisposition. Initially, the pancreas may produce extra insulin to overcome the resistance, but over time, it may become less effective at doing so. Lifestyle changes, such as healthy eating and regular physical activity, are important for managing type 2 diabetes. Some people with type 2 diabetes may also require oral medications or insulin therapy to help control their blood sugar levels.

In both types of diabetes, uncontrolled high blood sugar levels can lead to a range of complications, including heart disease, stroke, kidney disease, nerve damage, and vision problems. Therefore, maintaining proper blood sugar control is essential for the long-term health of individuals with diabetes.

In summary, the glucose molecule, insulin, the pancreas, and beta cells are integral components of the body's blood sugar regulation system. In a normal person, they work together seamlessly to keep blood sugar levels within a healthy range. In type 1 diabetes, the immune system attacks beta cells, leading to a lack of insulin production, while in type 2 diabetes, the body becomes resistant to insulin's effects. Both types of diabetes require careful management and monitoring to prevent complications and maintain overall health.

Methi Seeds for Diabetes in Telugu || Dr. Deepthi Kareti
description:
What are the benefits of fenugreek seeds?
Fenugreek may aid metabolic conditions, such as diabetes ( 8 ). In one study, people with type 2 diabetes who took 5 g of fenugreek seed powder twice daily for 2 months experienced a reduction in fasting blood sugar levels, belly fat, body mass index, and hemoglobin A1c, a marker of long-term blood sugar control ( 9 ).

Can I use fenugreek seeds daily?
Dosing. Fenugreek seed powder has most often been used by adults in doses of 5-10 grams by mouth daily for up to 3 years. Fenugreek seed extract has most often been used in doses of 0.6-1.2 grams by mouth daily.

Does fenugreek have side effects?
Potential side effects of fenugreek include diarrhea, nausea, and other digestive tract symptoms and rarely, dizziness and headaches. Large doses may cause a harmful drop in blood sugar. Fenugreek can cause allergic reactions in some people.

Can fenugreek reduce belly fat?
Methi or fenugreek has Galactomannan, a component that helps in curbing appetite and improving metabolism. Methi also helps in digestion and promotes belly fat loss. You can add methi to stir-fries, mathris, parathas and a range of delicious dishes.

title: El TRUCO para REDUCIR AZÚCAR NATURALMENTE y prevenir DIABETES
description:
¿Sabías que mantener un nivel adecuado de azúcar en sangre es fundamental para evitar múltiples enfermedades? La glucosa elevada en sangre puede desencadenar problemas de salud serios, como trombosis cerebrales, infarto, hígado graso, ansiedad, síndrome de ovario poliquístico, prediabetes, diabetes, y hasta cáncer. Controlar los niveles de glucosa es más que una necesidad; es una forma de proteger tu salud en general.

¡Baja el azúcar en sangre SIN REMEDIOS! | Diabetes tratamiento natural. Lo primero y más importante es adoptar una alimentación consciente. Eliminar de tu dieta los azúcares, jarabes, mieles y alimentos altos en almidón como arroz, papa, yuca, plátano, y pasta es esencial. Optar por carbohidratos provenientes de vegetales y consumir alimentos en un orden que priorice los vegetales puede hacer una gran diferencia.

¿Por qué es vital reducir el consumo de ciertos tipos de carbohidratos? Al consumir primero vegetales y luego proteínas, permites que tu cuerpo maneje mejor los niveles de glucosa. Si aún tienes hambre después de una comida, es mejor optar por más proteínas en lugar de carbohidratos adicionales. Este enfoque no solo ayuda a mantener los niveles de glucosa bajos, sino que también te deja más satisfecho y con menos probabilidad de tener picos de glucosa después de comer.

¿Cómo puede ayudarte el ejercicio en la regulación del azúcar en sangre? Combinar ejercicios de fuerza con cardio es la estrategia más eficaz. El ejercicio de fuerza ayuda a mejorar la capacidad de tus músculos para utilizar glucosa, lo que reduce la cantidad circulante en tu sangre. Mantener una buena masa muscular es crucial, ya que el músculo es uno de los principales consumidores de glucosa en el cuerpo.

¿Qué otros factores debes considerar para controlar tu glucosa? El manejo del estrés es fundamental. El estrés, tanto mental como físico, puede aumentar los niveles de glucosa en sangre al elevar hormonas como el cortisol y la adrenalina. Aprender a manejar el estrés eficazmente puede prevenir estos picos de glucosa y ayudarte a mantener un metabolismo saludable.

¿Existen suplementos que pueden ayudarte a manejar mejor la glucosa? Sí, algunos suplementos como el vinagre de manzana, la berberina, el inositol y el magnesio han demostrado ser útiles para mejorar los niveles de glucosa. Sin embargo, es crucial que cualquier suplemento se tome bajo supervisión médica y como complemento a una dieta saludable y un estilo de vida activo.

title: Is Upma Good For Diabetes Patients || Dr. Deepthi Kareti
description:
1.What Are the Health Benefits of Eating Upma?
Some of the major health advantages of eating upma are as follows:

Just a bowl of upma is enough to make you feel full for quite a long time. That's because upma is a slow-digesting food that keeps cravings at bay.
Minerals such as phosphorus, magnesium and zinc in rava upma are good at improving bones, kidney function and the nervous system.
Upma is one of the best sources of iron that prevents anaemia while boosting blood circulation.
Upma is free of harmful cholesterol and thus works wonders for heart health. It maintains healthy heart pumping and proper blood flow, offering its nutritional elements.
Upma made from wheat rava contains a lot of potassium which is healthy for the kidneys.

2.Is upma good for diabetes?
Yes, Upma is good for diabetes because it is high in nutritional value and contains fibre that maintains proper blood sugar levels.

3.Can I Eat Upma Every day?
Yes, you can consume upma every day.

4.Is sooji high in sugar?
glycemic index of sooji is low, around 66, indicating a slow and steady increase in blood glucose. The low GI index of suji makes it a potentially better option for individuals with diabetes.

5.‍Is sooji good for weight loss?
Yes, sooji is recommended for people who are trying to lose weight. 100 grams of unenriched sooji consists of approximately 360 calories and no cholesterol. Moreover, the high fiber content in this ingredient can promote slow digestion of food in the body. This leads to the person feeling full for a longer duration and curbs the frequent hunger pangs and intake of food. Suji also has a lot of iron, calcium, magnesium, zinc, and other vital nutrients that help maintain energy levels within the body.

What is Diabetes Mellitus?
description:
Diabetes Mellitus is a chronic type of endocrine disease, that occurs when your glucose level (blood sugar) is too high. It develops when your pancreas doesn’t make enough insulin or any at all, or when your body isn’t responding to the effects of insulin properly.

TYPES OF DIABETES👉 https://youtu.be/1waR5mvf590

GESTATIONAL DIABETES (Symptoms & Risk Factors)👉 https://youtu.be/nE3tN7Xu3sk

PREDIABETES (Symptoms & Risk Factors)👉 https://youtu.be/7T6Z1fGivkg

TYPE 2 DIABETES (Symptoms & Risk Factors)👉 https://youtu.be/IYqT-H9IZJs

TYPE 1 DIABETES (Symptoms & Risk Factors)👉 https://youtu.be/cxloy7Str8Q

TYPE 3C DIABETES & Other Forms Of Diabetes Mellitus👉 https://youtu.be/ntfXDUWQWDM

Diabetes affects people of all ages. Most forms of diabetes are chronic (lifelong), and all forms are manageable with medications and/or lifestyle changes.

The main cause of diabetes varies by type. But no matter what type of diabetes you have, it can lead to excess sugar in the blood. Too much sugar in the blood can lead to serious health problems.

📚Full note on Diabetes Mellitus [with pdf]: https://bit.ly/DiabetesNote

title: ¿Cómo revertir una diabetes o hiperglucemia?
description:
Hoy tenemos más herramientas que nunca para prevenir la diabetes y evitar los picos de azúcar en sangre o glucemia elevada.
Desde cambios en la alimentación hasta cambios en nuestros patrones de comida como el ayuno intermitente que han demostrado ser muy efectivos para reducir la glucemia en sangre.

¿Por qué debo evitar los picos de azúcar si no tengo diabetes?
Aún más prevalente que la diabetes es una condición llamada resistencia a la insulina que suele ser un paso previo a la diabetes que nos lleva a aumentar de peso y a aumentar nuestros niveles de insulina en sangre.
Incluso si no tengo resistencia a la insulina, las constantes variaciones de azúcar en sangre por una mala dieta nos llevan a más inflamación, mayor porcentaje de grasa corporal, peor estado de ánimo y menor expectativa de vida, con lo cuál utiliza alguno de los consejos de este video para mantener el azúcar en sangre estable.

00:34 ¿Que hace que varíe la glucemia?
02:37 ¿Qué sucede cuando nuestra glucemia es elevada en sangre?
03:42 Ejercicios y suplementos para bajar la glucemia, inflamación y riesgo cardiovascular
08:48 ¿Qué hábitos puedo tener para bajar la glucemia?

Get enough sleep.

Is 200 blood sugar normal after eating?
Less than 140 mg/dL (7.8 mmol/L ) after two hours is considered healthy. 140 to 199 mg/dL (7.8 mmol/L and 11.0 mmol/L ) is diagnosed as prediabetes. 200 mg/dL (11.1 mmol/L ) or higher after two hours suggests diabetes.

Does walking help in diabetes?
This is because walking: helps the body use insulin better. helps relieve stress which can affect your blood sugar levels, and reduce symptoms of depression and anxiety.

Which exercises reduce blood sugar?
Swimming helps lower your blood sugar levels

title: Tips to Lower your Blood Sugar in Telugu || Deepthi Kareti
description:
Can walking reduce sugar?
Just 2 minutes of walking after eating can help blood sugar, study says. Getting up and moving after you eat -- even if it's only for two minutes -- can help control blood sugar levels,

What is the best way to control sugar?
Drinking water and staying hydrated is important for managing blood sugar, also known as blood glucose. “Water helps your kidneys filter out excess sugar through urine,” says Khan. “So, the more hydrated you are, the more urine production you'll have, which flushes out sugar in the body.”

How can I control eating sugar?
Drink a glass of water. Some people say that dehydration can cause cravings.
Eat a fruit. Having a piece of fruit may help satisfy sugar cravings for some people.
Avoid artificial sweeteners.
Eat more protein.
Talk to a friend.
Sleep well.
Avoid excess stress.

What are no sugar foods?
Vegetables. Compared to fruits, most vegetables contain less sugar.
Meat. Seafood, pork, beef, and chicken are all sugar-free.
Beans, Nuts, Lentils. If you don't eat meat, soybeans, lentils, beans, nuts, and seeds are great sugar-free, high-protein foods.

title: HbA1c Test For Diabetes In Telugu || Dr. Deepthi Kareti
description:
1.Does walking reduce HbA1c?
walking is effective in decreasing HbA1c, by an average of 0.50% (95% CI, 0.70–0.21%), in diabetic patients.

2.Is HbA1c 7.0 normal?
For people without diabetes, the normal range for the hemoglobin A1c level is between 4% and 5.6%.

3.What are the tips to lower HbA1c levels?
 Lose extra weight,Eat a balanced, healthy diet, Get more physical activity. Exercise can help to lose weight, lower blood sugar and boost sensitivity to insulin
Limit alcohol intake and Quit smoking.

4.Do I need to fast for a HbA1c test?
  You do not need to fast for the HbA1c test.

5.What should I avoid if my HbA1c is high?
 sugar,sweets,juices and carbohydrate rich food

description:
1.How healthy is idli?
Idlis are considered healthy when consumed as a part of a balanced diet. The fermentation process helps in the production of probiotics which help in balancing out gut microflora making it beneficial for immunity. They are easy to digest, and light on the stomach.
2.Is idli high in protein?
It is a rich source of protein, carbs, iron,
3.Can I eat idli every day?
Of course, you can. 
4.Does idli digest easily?
The fermentation process in idli predigests the complex starches present in rice and dal. As a result, the final product becomes much easier to digest, making it an ideal choice for those with sensitive stomachs or digestive issues.

title: ¡PELIGRO! ⚠️ MITOS de la DIABETES | OJO a estos Síntomas | Mr. Doctor & Doctor Vic
description:
¡Suscríbete y Activa las notificaciones! 
#Diabetes #Mitos #Azucar #diabetestipoII #MrDoctor #MedicinaViral #DoctorVic

Contacto: dr.vic@sunsetsocial.mx 

Escúchanos Spoti: https://spotify.openinapp.co/vpfdf

En este episodio de medicina viral invitamos a Mr.Doctor el Dr. Octavio Arroyo médico cirujano especialista en Medicina Interna, nos hablará de todos los mitos de la diabetes.

Mr.Doctor: https://youtube.com/@mrdoctor?si=ha3Q17nFFAYLJRBu

La DIABETES MELLITUS es una enfermedad en la que las células pierden la capacidad de captar y utilizar la glucosa como fuente de energía debido a que el sistema de la
INSULINA no funciona correctamente.
La diabetes puede ser increíblemente compleja y para la que existen muchos tipos distintos cada una con sus particularidades: diabetes tipo 1, tipo 2, diabetes tipo MODY, diabetes gestacional, diabetes neonatal... ¿Qué caracteriza entonces a la diabetes? ¿Hay motivos reales para llamarla la epidemia del siglo XXI?

Los síntomas de inicio de diabetes son provocados por la inflamación en el cuerpo. Todas estas son señales de que puedes tener prediabetes: fatiga, cansancio, dolores crónicos en los músculos y articulaciones, alteraciones de la memoria y concentración, infeccion urinaria.

La diabetes y la prediabetes pueden tratarse de manera natural, y los mejores alimentos para un diabético son las frutas y verduras con efecto antioxidante y antinflamatorio como la granada, las moras, los arándanos y los frutos rojos.

title: Diabetes mellitus. Tipos, síntomas, diagnóstico y tratamiento. Clínica Universidad de Navarra
description:
La diabetes es una afección crónica que aparece cuando el páncreas no es capaz de producir suficiente insulina o cuando el organismo no consigue utilizar la insulina que produce.

Se caracteriza fundamentalmente por la presencia de elevadas concentraciones de glucosa en sangre (hiperglucemia). Esto se produce debido a la alteración en la acción de la insulina o en ausencia de esta hormona, que es producida en el páncreas para permitir la captación de glucosa por los tejidos, que la utilizan como combustible.

Hay que conseguir un buen control de la glucemia para prevenir las complicaciones a medio-largo plazo del diabético: daño neurológico, renal, etc

El Dr. Zubiría, endocrinólogo de la Clínica Universidad de Navarra nos cuenta todo sobre la diabetes mellitus.

title: Why ADHD, Autism, and Diabetes Have Skyrocketed
description:
Our children are being fed toxic chemicals, ultra-processed junk, and excessive sugar—all under the guise of “nutrition.”

The science is clear: many of the additives found in school meals are linked to cancer, reproductive harm, hormone disruption, and DNA damage. They serve no nutritional purpose—only to extend shelf life, enhance color, and modify texture.

Meanwhile, childhood obesity has increased by 400% in the last 40 years. Type 2 diabetes in adolescents has surged 200% in just two decades. 1 in 4 teenagers has prediabetes. ADHD diagnoses have skyrocketed 800%, and autism rates have gone from 4 in 10,000 to 1 in 36.

This isn’t normal. This isn’t genetic. This is a food problem.

Europe bans thousands of chemicals we allow in our food. Why aren’t we asking why?

Our kids deserve better. That’s why I’m fighting for SB 314, a bill that bans some of the worst chemicals from public school meals—because no child should be forced to eat food that puts their health at risk.

It’s time to wake up. Food is the root cause of our crisis—and it must be the solution.

what is remission ?
Remission is depending on diet, You can take low calorie diet. 
What is the cause of glucotoxicity?

Untreated high blood sugar can lead to a condition called glucotoxicity . It's caused by damaged beta cells. Beta cells help your body create and release a hormone called insulin. Insulin pulls sugar (also called glucose) out of your blood so your cells can use it for energy.

title: Superfoods for Type 2 Diabetes || Dr. Deepthi Kareti
description:
What food is best for diabetic patients?
Fruits and vegetables.
Whole grains, such as whole wheat, brown rice, barley, quinoa, and oats.
Proteins, such as lean meats, chicken, turkey, fish, eggs, nuts, beans, lentils, and tofu.
Nonfat or low-fat dairy, such as milk, yogurt, and cheese.

What foods reduce blood sugar?
Eating protein — from meat and fish, tofu, nuts, eggs, and cheese — with carbs can slow that flow of glucose and help stabilize blood sugar. “Proteins hold back the sugars, slow them down, and then release them into the bloodstream slowly so that your blood sugar stays in a more normal range,

Amla Juice. Amla juice is a healthy choice for people with diabetes.

title: Control Diabetes Without Medicine | Diabetes Control Tips | Diabetes Diet | Cholesterol Health Show
description:
Diabetes happens when your body isn't able to take up sugar (glucose) into its cells and use it for energy. This results in a build up of extra sugar in your bloodstream.

Mismanagement of diabetes can lead to serious consequences, causing damage to a wide range of your body's organs and tissues — including your heart, kidneys, eyes and nerves.

The types of diabetes are:

Type 1 diabetes: 

This type is an autoimmune disease, meaning your body attacks itself. In this case, the insulin-producing cells in your pancreas are destroyed. Up to 10% of people who have diabetes have Type 1. It’s usually diagnosed in children and young adults (but can develop at any age). It was once better known as “juvenile” diabetes. People with Type 1 diabetes need to take insulin every day. This is why it is also called insulin-dependent diabetes.

Type 2 diabetes: With this type, your body either doesn’t make enough insulin or your body’s cells don’t respond normally to the insulin. This is the most common type of diabetes. Up to 95% of people with diabetes have Type 2. It usually occurs in middle-aged and older people. Other common names for Type 2 include adult-onset diabetes and insulin-resistant diabetes. Your parents or grandparents may have called it “having a touch of sugar.”

Prediabetes: This type is the stage before Type 2 diabetes. Your blood glucose levels are higher than normal but not high enough to be officially diagnosed with Type 2 diabetes.
Gestational diabetes: This type develops in some women during their pregnancy. Gestational diabetes usually goes away after pregnancy. However, if you have gestational diabetes you're at higher risk of developing Type 2 diabetes later on in life.

There are several causes of sleep problems for people with type 2 diabetes, including obstructive sleep apnea, pain or discomfort, restless legs syndrome, and increased nighttime urination.

Poor sleep.

title: Is Curd Good For Diabetes? in Telugu || Dr. Deepthi Kareti
description:
Does curd raise blood sugar levels?
Portion size is the key to managing your sugar levels. While curd is considered to be a diabetes-friendly option, you need to keep the portion size in check. Consuming excessive amounts of curd on a regular basis can lead to a spike in your blood sugar levels.

Is ghee good for diabetes?
The fatty acids present in ghee help metabolize and balance high blood sugar levels in the body. Therefore, diabetes patients are often recommended to add a teaspoon of ghee to their diet. proven to balance blood sugar level as the fatty acids present in it helps in glucose metabolism.

Can we eat daily curd with sugar?
Sugar enhances the taste of curd, making it more appealing to those who find plain curd too tart or sour. However, it is important to note that consuming curd with added sugar regularly may not be suitable for everyone, especially those looking to manage their weight or blood sugar levels.

Is curd good for diabetic patients?
Curd and yogurt is rich in protein, calcium, magnesium and other vitamins and considered as beneficial for diabetics.

For all your queries please do watch this video completely and meet Dr. Deepthi Kareti at Immense Diabetes Care Centre.
Where she can help you put together a diet based on your health goals, tastes and lifestyle. she can also talk with you about how to improve your eating habits, such as choosing portion sizes that suit the needs for your size and activity level.

title: 5 DICAS DE COMO REVERTER o PRÉ-DIABETES e o DIABETES TIPO 2 NATURALMENTE
description:
5 DICAS DE COMO REVERTER o PRÉ-DIABETES e o DIABETES TIPO 2 NATURALMENTE (assim como os sintomas de muito açúcar no sangue que você não pode ignorar)
💕INSCREVA-SE:  www.youtube.com/@CardioDF?sub_confirmation=1
✅ COMPARTILHE o vídeo no WHATSAPP e vamos ajudar mais pessoas!
————
⏱️TIMESTAMPS:
0:00- 5 dicas para reverter o pré-diabetes e o diabetes tipo 2
1:13- Como reverter o pré-diabetes e o diabetes tipo 2?
1:17- O que é pré-diabetes?
2:07- Sabe quantos diabéticos existiam em 1980 no mundo?
2:47- Por que subiu tanto o número de diabéticos recentemente? 
3:14- Por que a obesidade tem a ver com diabetes?
4:06- Todo mundo que tem resistência à insulina é obeso?
5:16- Quais os níveis considerados normais de glicemia?
5:33- Glicemia de jejum
5:46- Exame de tolerância à glicose oral
6:01- Hb glicada
6:50- Quais os sintomas de diabetes?
7:03- Como evitar que o pré-diabético vire diabético?
7:33- Quando seria considerado reversão do diabetes?
8:17- 5 dicas de como sair do pré-diabetes ou como sair do diabetes
8:24- 1.  Dica para reverter o diabetes: Evite comidas industrializadas
9:25- 2. Dica para sair do diabetes ou pré diabetes: Faça atividades físicas!
10:25- 3. Dica para reverter o diabetes: Durma melhor
10:46- 4. Dica para sair do diabetes ou pré diabetes: Reduza o estresse
11:50- 5.Dica para reverter o diabetes: Perca peso
12:28- Como perder peso e reverter diabetes? 1a dica: Dietas de baixas calorias
13:04- Como perder peso e reverter o diabetes: 2a dica. Dietas com restrição de carboidratos
14:59- Como perder peso e reverter a diabetes: 3a dica. Cirurgia bariátrica
16:18- Importante para quem tem diabetes ou pré-diabetes!

O diabetes  tipo 2 é uma doença comum no Brasil, afeta mais de 15 milhões de pessoas. E o pré-diabetes afeta assustadores 40 milhões de brasileiros. No diabetes o corpo não produz insulina suficiente ou não usa adequadamente a insulina que produz. Insulina é um hormônio produzido pelo pâncreas e ajuda a glicose (o açúcar) a entrar nas células para fornecer energia. No mundo o índice de diabéticos está aumentando em um taxa alarmante, saltando de 108 milhões de pessoas no mundo em 1980 para incríveis 507 milhões em 2022. Felizmente há maneiras para reverter a pré-diabetes e evitar que o indivíduo o vire diabético ( e em alguns casos é possível reverter o diabetes).

Nesse vídeo, apresentamos as 5 dicas de como reverter o pré-diabetes e o diabetes tipo 2 naturalmente, além de explicar sobre os sintomas de diabetes, a relação entre obesidade e resistência insulínica e sobre exames de sangue importantes para o diabetes, como glicemia capilar, hb glicada, insulina basal, homa e curva glicêmica.

title: 9 SINAIS de DIABETES que acontecem à NOITE | PRIMEIROS SINTOMAS DE DIABETES e PRÉ-DIABETES (cuidado)
description:
9 SINAIS estranhos de DIABETES que acontecem à NOITE | PRIMEIROS SINTOMAS DE DIABETES e PRÉ-DIABETES
💕💕INSCREVA-SE:  https://bit.ly/2IUdn43​​​    
✅ COMPARTILHE o vídeo no WHATSAPP e vamos ajudar mais pessoas! 

0:00- 9 SINAIS estranhos de DIABETES que acontecem à NOITE | PRIMEIROS SINTOMAS DE DIABETES e PRÉ-DIABETES
1:05- 9 SINAIS estranhos de DIABETES que acontecem à NOITE | PRIMEIROS SINTOMAS DE DIABETES e PRÉ-DIABETES
1:19- O que é pré-diabetes?
1:59- Quais os 9 sintomas que os diabéticos sentem à noite?
2:09- 1º sintoma de diabetes à noite
3:04- 2º sinal que aparece para o diabético à noite
4:27- 3º sintoma de diabetes que acontecem à noite
5:26- 4º sinal de diabetes à noite
6:20- 5º sintoma que aparece à noite para o diabético
7:20- 6º sintoma de diabetes que aparecem à noite
8:05- 7º sintoma noturno de diabetes
8:37- 8º sintoma de diabetes que acontece à noite
9:18- 9º sintoma noturno de diabetes
10:00- Como prevenir que um pré- diabético não vire diabético?

Você sabia que o diabetes pode se manifestar de diferentes formas?
Existem sintomas muito frequentes que aparecem à noite que podem indicar que a pessoa pode estar com pré-diabetes ou diabetes inicial.  E isso tem um grande valor, porque muita gente com diabetes não sabe que tem a doença. Quanto mais cedo se descobre, melhor. 
Se a pessoa for pré-diabética pode evitar que torne diabético. E se for diabético inicial, a pessoa pode evitar que a doença complique. É importante que a glicose do sangue seja bem controlada para diminuir o risco de desenvolver complicações associadas ao diabetes.

Quando a pessoa tem diabetes, o corpo não produz insulina suficiente, que é um hormônio que faz o açúcar entrar para dentro da célula, ou não a utiliza adequadamente, sendo pouco sensível ou resistente à insulina.

title: Why ADHD, Autism, and Diabetes Have Skyrocketed
description:
Our children are being fed toxic chemicals, ultra-processed junk, and excessive sugar—all under the guise of “nutrition.”

The science is clear: many of the additives found in school meals are linked to cancer, reproductive harm, hormone disruption, and DNA damage. They serve no nutritional purpose—only to extend shelf life, enhance color, and modify texture.

Meanwhile, childhood obesity has increased by 400% in the last 40 years. Type 2 diabetes in adolescents has surged 200% in just two decades. 1 in 4 teenagers has prediabetes. ADHD diagnoses have skyrocketed 800%, and autism rates have gone from 4 in 10,000 to 1 in 36.

This isn’t normal. This isn’t genetic. This is a food problem.

Europe bans thousands of chemicals we allow in our food. Why aren’t we asking why?

Our kids deserve better. That’s why I’m fighting for SB 314, a bill that bans some of the worst chemicals from public school meals—because no child should be forced to eat food that puts their health at risk.

It’s time to wake up. Food is the root cause of our crisis—and it must be the solution.

Top 10 Best Breakfasts Every Diabetic Must Know! Best Breakfast For A Diabetic
description:
Top 10 Best Breakfasts Every Diabetic Must Know! Best Breakfast For A Diabetic

Starting your day with the right breakfast is crucial for diabetics who want to maintain stable blood sugar levels and avoid energy crashes. Many people make the mistake of consuming high-carb, processed foods in the morning, which can lead to dangerous glucose spikes. However, there are plenty of delicious and nutritious options that help diabetics stay full and energized without compromising their health. From protein-packed eggs and fiber-rich oatmeal to Greek yogurt with berries and low-carb smoothies, these meals provide a balanced combination of protein, healthy fats, and slow-digesting carbohydrates. For diabetics, making smart food choices in the morning can set the tone for the entire day, promoting better insulin sensitivity and long-term well-being. Choosing whole foods, nutrient-dense ingredients, and avoiding added sugars are key strategies that all diabetics should incorporate into their daily routine. By prioritizing healthy breakfasts, diabetics can take control of their diet, improve their overall health, and enjoy meals that are both satisfying and beneficial.

If you found this information helpful, don’t forget to like, subscribe, and comment below! Share your favorite diabetic-friendly breakfast and let us know how you manage your morning meals! 🚀💬

non-starchy vegetables.

What fruit reduces insulin resistance?
Pears are our favorite fruit for insulin resistance because research shows a relationship between eating them and lower risk of type 2 diabetes. Apples, berries and citrus fruits are also great options to regularly include in your diet.

What is a good breakfast for insulin resistance?
Start your day with a nutritious breakfast with protein, healthy fats, and fiber. Breakfast ideas for insulin resistance include: Vegetable omelet: Make a veggie stuffed omelet with eggs, spinach, tomatoes, and red bell peppers.

Does lemon reduce insulin resistance?
Lemons are good for you, and many alternative practitioners contend that they can help stabilize blood sugar. Others believe that lemons can reverse insulin resistance, meaning the body's inability to respond to insulin—the hormone produced by the pancreas tasked with regulating blood sugar.

title: Diabetes Control Tips | Sugar Control Tips | Control Diabetes without Medicine | Health Show
description:
Diabetes is a chronic disease that occurs either when the pancreas does not produce enough insulin or when the body cannot effectively use the insulin it produces. Insulin is a hormone that regulates blood glucose. Hyperglycaemia, also called raised blood glucose or raised blood sugar, is a common effect of uncontrolled diabetes and over time leads to serious damage to many of the body's systems, especially the nerves and blood vessels.

In 2014, 8.5% of adults aged 18 years and older had diabetes. In 2019, diabetes was the direct cause of 1.5 million deaths and 48% of all deaths due to diabetes occurred before the age of 70 years. Another 460 000 kidney disease deaths were caused by diabetes, and raised blood glucose causes around 20% of cardiovascular deaths (1).

Between 2000 and 2019, there was a 3% increase in age-standardized mortality rates from diabetes. In lower-middle-income countries, the mortality rate due to diabetes increased 13%.

By contrast, the probability of dying from any one of the four main noncommunicable diseases (cardiovascular diseases, cancer, chronic respiratory diseases or diabetes) between the ages of 30 and 70 decreased by 22% globally between 2000 and 2019.

MEDICAL ANIMATION TRANSCRIPT: Es posible que a usted o a alguien que conoce le hayan diagnosticado diabetes. Este video le mostrará qué es y por qué es importante mantenerla bajo control. Las personas pueden tener diferentes síntomas con la diabetes. Los síntomas comunes incluyen tener mucha hambre, tener mucha sed, orinar con frecuencia, fatiga, visión borrosa y cicatrización lenta de las heridas. Las personas con diabetes tipo 1 también pueden experimentar pérdida de peso. Los síntomas de la diabetes tipo 1 se desarrollan repentinamente. Es posible que puedan hacer que una persona se sienta enferma. Por otro lado, los síntomas de la diabetes tipo 2 generalmente se desarrollan lentamente. Pueden ser lo suficientemente leves y por lo tanto difíciles de notar. Algunas personas no presentan ningún síntoma. Si no se trata el alto nivel de azúcar en su sangre, puede traer como consecuencia complicaciones potencialmente mortales; por ejemplo, se puede enfermar gravemente con una afección llamada cetoacidosis diabética, es más común en las personas con diabetes tipo 1. Así es como sucede: debido a que tiene demasiado poca o nada de insulina en su sangre, sus células no pueden recibir el azúcar que necesitan para energía. Como resultado, su cuerpo busca la energía en las grasas y las proteínas. Cuando su cuerpo descompone las grasas, ciertas sustancias conocidas como cuerpos de cetonas se acumulan en su sangre. Esto provoca una afección llamada cetosis. Si las cetonas se acumulan de manera peligrosa en altos niveles en su sangre, quizá desarrolle cetoacidosis diabética o DKA, esto puede provocar un coma o la muerte. Si su nivel de azúcar en sangre cae demasiado, quizás experimente una complicación seria de la diabetes llamada hipoglucemia. Una forma común de que pase esto es al tomar demasiados medicamentos para diabéticos o no comer. Demasiada insulina o medicamentos orales para diabéticos puede provocar que demasiada azúcar vaya a sus células. Entonces, su nivel de azúcar en sangre cae demasiado. Ciertos órganos, como el cerebro, necesitan un suministro constante de energía para funcionar correctamente. Debido a que la principal fuente de energía del cerebro es el azúcar, este es el primer órgano afectado al disminuir los niveles de azúcar. Cuando las células de su cerebro, llamadas neuronas, no reciben suficiente azúcar, comienzan a funcionar mal. Puede tener síntomas como nerviosismo, temblor y confusión. Si el azúcar en su sangre continúa bajando, su cerebro no tiene suficiente energía para funcionar correctamente. Esto puede provocar convulsiones o un coma diabético. Si el nivel de azúcar en su sangre permanece alto con el tiempo, pueden ocurrir problemas de salud a largo plazo. Entre ellos se incluyen enfermedad cardíaca y vascular, ataque de apoplejía, ceguera, daño a los nervios, enfermedad renal y amputación del pie o de la pierna. Si tiene preguntas sobre la diabetes o los medicamentos para la misma, hable con su médico. Es importante que tome sus medicamentos como se lo indicó su médico. Cuéntele sobre cualquier efecto secundario que tenga.

ANH16176es

title: Foods to Help With Type 2 Diabetes  #diabetes #type2diabetes #doctortips #healthtips #doctor
description:
Which of these foods do you eat? They are all powerhouses!

Foods that can help with diabetes are foods that contain soluble fiber (which help lowers blood sugar), healthy fat and protein (which slows the digestion of carbohydrates in a meal to reduce blood sugar spikes). The omega 3 fatty acids in salmon may help reduce insulin resistance and inflammation!

Other things you can do to help lower blood sugar is take a short walk (or even do calf raises or air squats) after a meal, weight train at least twice per week, eat the protein in your meal first and the carbohydrate last, track your carbohydrate macros and adjust how much carbs you are eating based on your blood sugar levels, and avoid eating just carbs at any given meal or snack (be sure to pair with a protein and/or fat).

I eat all of these foods regularly, but my favorite is salmon!! What is your fave from this list??

#diabetes #type2diabetes #doctortips #healthtips #doctor

title: Top 9 Best Vegetables For Diabetics to Eat! (Lower Blood Sugar)
description:
Top 9 Best Vegetables For Diabetics to Eat! (Lower Blood Sugar)

Managing diabetes effectively requires more than just avoiding certain foods—it’s about making smart dietary choices that actively support blood sugar control. For diabetics, incorporating the right vegetables into daily meals can significantly improve insulin sensitivity and overall metabolic health. Some vegetables, like Brussels sprouts, spinach, and broccoli, are rich in fiber, antioxidants, and bioactive compounds that help stabilize glucose levels.

For diabetics, vegetables like cabbage, tomatoes, and cucumbers provide essential vitamins and minerals that support heart health and reduce inflammation. Avocados, packed with healthy fats, are another excellent choice for improving insulin sensitivity and preventing blood sugar spikes. These nutrient-dense foods help diabetics regulate glucose absorption while reducing the risk of diabetes-related complications.

Choosing fresh, steamed, or lightly sautéed vegetables with healthy fats like olive oil can maximize their benefits. A well-balanced diet, along with regular exercise and stress management, is crucial for diabetics seeking stable blood sugar levels.

description:
Top 3 Vitamins Every Diabetic MUST Take! (Lower Blood Sugar)

Managing diabetes isn’t just about cutting carbs and taking medications—diabetics need the right nutrients to truly control blood sugar and prevent complications. Many diabetics are unknowingly defVitamin D, Vitamin B12, and Folate (B9), which play essential roles in insulin function, nerve health, and inflammation control. Without these nutrients, diabetics may experience worsening neuropathy, fatigue, and higher risks of heart and kidney disease. Addressing these deficiencies can improve energy levels, reduce nerve pain, and support overall health. The right vitamins don’t replace medications, but they enhance the body’s ability to heal and regulate itself naturally. Every diabetic should focus on replenishing these essential nutrients through diet and supplementation to live a healthier, more vibrant life.

Symptomer diabetes
description:
Symptomer på diabetes varierer, både i rekkefølge og styrke. Generelt er symptomene på diabetes type 1 tydeligere enn ved diabetes type 2. Dette er de mest vanlige symptomene på diabetes:

• Trøtt og sliten: Fordi kroppen ikke tar opp energien fra mat og drikke
• Økt vannlating: Man tisser oftere fordi kroppen prøver å kvitte seg med det høye blodsukkeret. 
• Tørste: Økt vannlating fører igjen til økt tørste grunnet dehydrering. 
• Vektnedgang: Næringen fra mat og drikke tas ikke opp i kroppen, og kroppen vil da bruke fettceller som forbrenning. En annen årsak til vektnedgang er stort væsketap pga økt vannlating/økt diurese. 

Det finnes også mange andre symptomer på diabetes som ikke er så vanlige som de som vises i filmen. Ved diabetes type 1 vil man fortere bli alvorlig syk, mens ved diabetes type 2 kan man ha sykdommen lenge uten å oppdage det på grunn av svake symptomer. 
Det er enkelt å finne ut om man har diabetes ved å måle blodsukkeret hos legen.

Denne videoen er utviklet med økonomisk støtte fra Damstiftelsen.

title: TOP 6 Fish Every Diabetics Should Eat! (Lower Blood Sugar)
description:
TOP 6 Fish Every Diabetics Should Eat! (Lower Blood Sugar)

Fish is one of the best foods for diabetics, offering essential nutrients that support blood sugar control, heart health, and overall well-being. The top six fish for diabetics include tuna, salmon, herring, sardines, tilapia, and mackerel. These fish are packed with high-quality protein, omega-3 fatty acids, and vital vitamins like B12 and D, which help reduce inflammation, improve insulin sensitivity, and lower the risk of heart disease.

For diabetics, maintaining steady blood sugar levels is crucial, and fish provides a lean, low-carb protein source that prevents glucose spikes while keeping energy levels stable. Omega-3-rich fish like salmon and mackerel also enhance cardiovascular health, reducing the risk of complications associated with diabetes. Additionally, sardines and herring offer a powerful boost of selenium and vitamin D, which further aid in blood sugar management and nerve function.

By incorporating these nutrient-packed fish into a balanced diet, diabetics can take control of their health naturally. Whether grilled, baked, or added to fresh salads, fish is a delicious way to nourish the body.

If you found this helpful, don’t forget to like, subscribe, and comment below with your favorite fish for diabetes management! 🐟💙

title: Type 1 diabetes | Control diabetes without medicine | Sugar control tips | The Health Show
description:
Type 1 diabetes, once known as juvenile diabetes or insulin-dependent diabetes, is a chronic condition. In this condition, the pancreas makes little or no insulin. Insulin is a hormone the body uses to allow sugar (glucose) to enter cells to produce energy.

Different factors, such as genetics and some viruses, may cause type 1 diabetes. Although type 1 diabetes usually appears during childhood or adolescence, it can develop in adults.

Even after a lot of research, type 1 diabetes has no cure. Treatment is directed toward managing the amount of sugar in the blood using insulin, diet and lifestyle to prevent complications.

title: 3 Buah yang Sebaiknya Tidak Dikonsumsi Penderita Diabetes
description:
Konsumsi buah-buahan dapat mencukupi kebutuhan nutrisi dan serat pada penderita diabetes. Namun, diabetes tidak boleh makan buah apa? Ternyata, penderita diabetes perlu menghindari jenis buah dengan kandungan gula yang tinggi, seperti semangka, nanas, dan pisang yang terlalu matang.

Pasalnya, mengonsumsi beberapa jenis buah tersebut dalam jumlah yang berlebihan dapat meningkatkan kadar gula darah sehingga risiko komplikasi diabetes juga akan meningkat. Untuk lebih jelasnya, ketahui beberapa jenis buah yang dilarang untuk penderita diabetes berikut ini.

Penderita diabetes umumnya tidak perlu menghindari makan buah karena dapat menjadi bagian dari diet seimbang. Dilansir dari Medical News Today, buah memiliki vitamin, mineral, serat, dan karbohidrat yang baik untuk kesehatan, termasuk pada penderita diabetes.

Bahkan, beberapa jenis buah dapat membantu untuk menurunkan risiko diabetes pada orang-orang sehat.

title: No IGNORE Estos Síntomas Nocturnos de Diabetes
description:
Vea estos 12 síntomas de niveles altos de azúcar en sangre que pueden ocurrir mientras duerme.

En el video de hoy, el Dr. Veller profundizará en los síntomas de la diabetes que suelen pasarse por alto y que se manifiestan por la noche. Desde frecuentes visitas al baño hasta sudores nocturnos inexplicables, estos signos pueden ser la forma que tiene el cuerpo de indicar un problema subyacente. Si usted o un ser querido sufre noches de insomnio, es fundamental reconocer estos 12 signos nocturnos de diabetes.

00:00 Orinar frecuentemente de noche 
01:05 Mucha sed por la noche
01:50 Síndrome de las piernas inquietas
03:15 Neuropatía diabética 
04:09 Calambres en las piernas (calambres nocturnos)
05:13 Sudor excesivo por la noche (sudoraciones nocturnas)
06:03 Reflujo ácido (reflujo gastroesofágico) 
08:11 Pesadillas
09:07 Palpitaciones por la noche 
09:40 Rechinar los dientes 
10:27 Dolor de cabeza por la noche

title: Top 7 Vitamins Every Diabetics MUST Take! (Lower Blood Sugar)
description:
Top 7 Vitamins Every Diabetics MUST Take! (Lower Blood Sugar)

Managing diabetes isn’t just about diet and medication—many diabetics overlook the importance of key vitamins. Deficiencies in essential nutrients like vitamin D, B12, magnesium, and chromium can make blood sugar harder to control, worsen nerve damage, and increase fatigue. For diabetics, restoring these vital nutrients can improve insulin function, reduce neuropathy symptoms, and enhance overall well-being. Alpha-lipoic acid and CoQ10 also play crucial roles in protecting the heart, boosting energy, and fighting oxidative stress. Many diabetics unknowingly suffer from nutrient depletion due to high blood sugar, frequent urination, and medication side effects. Addressing these deficiencies can be a game changer in diabetes management. If you're a diabetic struggling with blood sugar control, fatigue, or nerve pain, it’s time to focus on replenishing these essential vitamins.

If you found this information helpful, don’t forget to like, subscribe, and comment to help more people manage diabetes effectively!

title: Hva er diabetes?
description:
Insulin er et hormon som produseres i bukspyttkjertelen og som har som hovedoppgave å senke blodsukkeret. Insulinet fungerer som en «nøkkel» som låser opp cellene slik at glukose (sukker) kommer inn i cellene og kan brukes som energi i kroppen. 

Ved diabetes type 2, så produserer kroppen enten for lite insulin i forhold til behovet og/eller det insulinet som produseres virker dårligere i kroppen og klarer ikke å åpne opp cellene. Da blir sukkeret liggende i blodet, og fører til høyt blodsukker. 

Når man har fått diabetes type 1, så produserer ikke kroppen noe insulin og blodsukkeret blir veldig høyt. Man kan ha litt insulinproduksjon helt i starten av sykdomsforløpet, men denne produksjonen vil stoppe opp etter en tid. 
Diabetes type 1 og type 2 er to forskjellige sykdommer. Felles for dem begge er at svekket insulinfunksjon medfører høyt blodsukker og at sykdommene er kroniske.

Denne videoen er utviklet med økonomisk støtte fra Damstiftelsen.

Can Diabetes Cause Paralysis? in Telugu || Dr. Deepthi Kareti
description:
Why does diabetes cause paralysis?
Diabetic neuropathy, which is nerve damage caused by high blood sugar levels, can also cause muscle weakness or even paralysis in extreme cases. Furthermore, stroke risk is notably higher in individuals with diabetes.

Can you live a long life with diabetes?
Life expectancy is known as the number of years a person is expected to live. At age 50, life expectancy is 6 years shorter for people with type 2 diabetes than for people without diabetes. By meeting type 2 diabetes treatment goals, life expectancy can increase by 3 years, or for some, as much as 10 years.

Does diabetes damage nerves?
Diabetes can affect nerves that control movement, sensation and other functions. If you have diabetes, you can develop nerve problems at any time. Sometimes, neuropathy can be the first sign of diabetes. Significant nerve problems (clinical neuropathy) can develop within the first 10 years after a diabetes diagnosis.

What is end stage diabetes?
End-stage diabetes is a term for the advanced complications that can develop after many years of living with diabetes. These complications can include heart disease, kidney problems, and nerve damage.

title: ¡CUIDADO! Si apareció esto en tu Piel ¡Tienes diabetes!
description:
¡7 PRINCIPALES SIGNOS de DIABETES en la PIEL! (aparece si tienes DEMASIADA AZÚCAR en la sangre): una advertencia para quienes tienen prediabetes, diabetes o resistencia a la insulina.

La diabetes es una peligrosa enfermedad, que puede ser mortal. Pero no todas son malas noticias, ya que si la detectas precozmente puede ser curada o revertida por completo. 

En este video te muestro los síntomas más comunes de diabetes que aparecen en la piel cuando tienes el azúcar muy alto en la sangre. 

Estas manifestaciones son las primeras que surgen en los pacientes con diabetes tipo 2, o incluso en etapas tempranas, antes que la prediabetes se transforme en diabetes. 

También te explicaré cuáles de estos signos y síntomas cutáneos indican que tu diabetes está más avanzada o ya tienes alguna complicación, como daño en tus nervios (neuropatía), insuficiencia renal (nefropatía) o daño en tus ojos (retinopatía). Además, te doy consejos médicos para controlar tu diabetes y mejorar tu salud. 

Videos con fines educativos. 

Partes del video:
00:00 7 SIGNOS de ADVERTENCIA de DIABETES que APARECEN EN TU PIEL
01:32 Hongos que salen cuando tienes azúcar demasiado alta en sangre 
02:49 Los primeros síntomas de diabetes en etapas iniciales 
04:35 Cómo curar y revertir la diabetes 
06:05 ¿Te salió esto en la piel? ¡puede indicar diabetes tipo 2! 
07:31 Síntoma que indica Prediabetes
08:48 Síntomas peligrosos que indican diabetes avanzada 
12:41 Mensajes y consejos finales

title: ¡SEÑALES de ALARMA de DIABETES en la NOCHE!| PRIMEROS SÍNTOMAS de DIABETES
description:
¡No ignores estos síntomas que indican que tienes la glucemia muy alta de noche!
La diabetes es una peligrosa enfermedad, pero puede ser revertida y tiene cura con cambios saludables en tu estilo de vida, sobre todo cuando es detectada a tiempo, en etapa temprana. Muchas personas padecen de diabetes y no lo saben, porque los síntomas se confunden con otros problemas de salud. 

La diabetes es una condición en la cual el cuerpo no produce suficiente insulina, o hay resistencia a la insulina provocando azúcar muy alta en sangre (glucemia elevada). 

En este video te cuento los primeros signos y síntomas de diabetes que se manifiestan de noche y al dormir por tener la glucosa muy alta en sangre. También te explico las complicaciones que pueden aparecer cuando no se controla la diabetes y tienes la glucemia muy elevada.

Tener la glucosa alta de noche puede causar síntomas como aumento de la frecuencia para orinar (nocturia), tener mucha sed excesiva de noche, boca seca, calambres en las piernas y pies, hormigueos o entumecimiento en los pies. 
El síndrome de las piernas inquietas también puede ser una manifestación de diabetes en etapa temprana. 

Pesadillas y terrores nocturos, insomnio y apnea del sueño también son formas que la diabetes se manifiesta de noche. 

Conocer estos síntomas puede hacer que detectes la diabetes en etapa precoz, y puedas tratarla a tiempo para evitar sus complicaciones como la neuropatía. 

Existen remedios caseros y naturales para la diabetes muy efectivos:
-Vinagre de manzana orgánico con la madre: puedes tomar 1 cucharada diluido en agua todos los días en ayunas y antes de dormir. Esto ayuda a controlar los niveles de glucosa elevada en sangre y mejorar los picos de insulina. 
-Jugo de remolacha (betabel): tomar 250ml todos los días es excelente para mejorar la inflamación y la resistencia a la insulina, sobre todo cuando lo acompañas de ejercicio físico. 
-Jugo de granada: tomar 250ml o consumir una granada todos los días por 3-4 semanas mostró reducir la glucemia elevada. 

Video con fines educativos. 

00:00 Síntomas más frecuentes de diabetes en la noche
01:00 Número 1: orinar mucho de noche (nocturia)
03:22 Número 2: visión borrosa en la noche
04:16 Número 3: tener mucha sed de noche
05:13 Número 4: calambres en las piernas de noche
07:22 Número 5: Pesadillas y terrores nocturnos
08:26 Número 6: Piernas inquietas de noche y entumecimiento en los pies 
09:29 Número 7: apnea del sueño
10:33 1 cucharada de vinagre de manzana por día baja la glucosa en sangre
12:00 Qué tomar para controlar la diabetes: los mejores jugos naturales 

Bibliografía
Libro recomendado: "El superpoder de los alimentos"

डायबिटीज (शुगर) आधुनिक जीवनशैली की सबसे खराब बीमारियों में से एक है, किन्तु इसे नियंत्रित कर पूरी तरह खत्म करने के सफल उपाय हमने अपने हेल्थकेयर सेमिनार्स में बताए हैं और अब तक कई भाई-बहिन हाई डायबिटीज...जिनका शुगर लेवल 400 से 500 तक रहता था, नियमित रूप से इंसुलिन लेना पडता था...तेजी से वजन घट रहा था, वे आज इससे मुक्त हुए हैं और कई वर्षों बाद फलों के राजा आम का आनंद ले रहे हैं।
Exercise regularly. Regular exercise can help you get to and maintain a moderate weight and increase insulin sensitivity. Manage your carb intake. Increase your fiber intake. Drink water and stay hydrated. Implement portion control. Choose foods with a low glycemic index. Manage stress levels. Monitor your blood sugar.
*डायबिटीज यानी शुगर, यह सबसे खराब बीमारियों में से एक है।*
*कोरोना से मरने वालों में सबसे ज्यादा वे हैं, जिनको डायबिटीज थी।*
*ब्लैक फंगस के सर्वाधिक शिकार वे हैं, जिन्हें डायबिटीज है।*
*दूसरी बीमारियों से भी वही लोग ज्यादा मरते हैं, जिन्हें डायबिटीज होती है।*
*डायबिटीज को जड़मूल से खत्म करिए।*

title: 7 Simple Steps to Manage Type 2 Diabetes Without Medication
description:
7 Simple Steps to Manage Type 2 Diabetes Without Medication

Are you struggling with type 2 diabetes and looking for ways to manage it without medication? In this video, we’ll show you 7 simple steps to naturally lower your blood sugar and potentially get off diabetes medication! By making the right lifestyle changes, you can improve insulin sensitivity, stabilize blood sugar, and enhance your overall health. Watch now to learn actionable tips on how to take control of your blood sugar levels!


7 Steps Covered in This Video:
Consult Your Healthcare Provider – Get professional advice before making changes.
Buy a Blood Sugar Meter – Track your progress and stay on top of your blood sugar.
Stop Eating Dough-Based Products – Why you should cut out bread and pastries.
Stop Drinking Sugary Drinks – Learn why sugary drinks can be harmful and what to drink instead.
Eat More of The Golden Duo – Discover the power of healthy fats and proteins.
Eat the Right Ratios – Learn the ideal plate ratio for blood sugar control.
Avoid Snacking – Why reducing snacks can help stabilize blood sugar.

By following these steps, you can manage type 2 diabetes naturally and improve your health. Start making small changes today!
Don't forget to like, comment, and subscribe for more videos on healthy living and diabetes management!

title: Ramadan vs Diabetes  | Keep tracking your Blood Glucose level. #Diabetes #ramadan #health
Low Carb Foods for Healthy nails and Diabetes Control #healthyfood
Life With Diabetes #diabetes #diabetesmanagement
description:
We are not medical professionals, we simply LIVE  WITH  DIABETES! 

This is not a video to shame anyone who takes more medication than my husband or chooses to not control their diabetes with diet. I’m simply here to try to help those that are looking for answers.

Being diabetic is beyond difficult. It’s a little bit of a silent disease that can affect so many aspects of your life.

We’ve been living with it for probably about 20 years now. My husband is type 2. 

He has had many ups and downs of getting off track and his A1c going up.  He has a Doctor who has full faith in him that he can control as much as he can by his nutrition. 

He is on very little medication, but he’s unable to get off of those. And that’s OK! He’s doing everything he can.

He does have to keep his weight down. 

A constant glucose moderate there is crucial at least according to us. It’s pretty frustrating because insurance companies will deny them. For us, the knowledge has been in the power of knowing exactly what food did exactly what to his glucose levels. Even exercise or different levels of hydration will do to his glucose levels. 

In the comments you can find the list of foods that he chooses to eat. And a list of foods that he chooses to not eat. I hope this helps!

#diabetes #a1c #glucoselevels #livingwithdiabetes #type2diabetes

title: Top 6 Protein Sources for Diabetes You NEED to Eat! (Balance Blood Sugar)
description:
📌 Top 6 Protein Sources for Diabetes You NEED to Eat! (Balance Blood Sugar)

🥩 Is your diet helping or hurting your blood sugar?
Many people focus on cutting carbs, but protein plays a crucial role in blood sugar control. The right protein sources stabilize glucose, improve insulin function, and keep you full longer—helping to prevent cravings and energy crashes.

🚨 But not all proteins are diabetes-friendly. Some increase inflammation and insulin resistance, making blood sugar control harder. In this video, I reveal the 6 BEST protein sources for diabetes that will help balance blood sugar naturally.

🌟 What You’ll Learn in This Video:
✅ The 6 BEST protein sources for stable blood sugar 🥩
✅ How protein boosts insulin sensitivity and prevents glucose spikes 🚀
✅ The worst protein sources that trigger blood sugar crashes ❌
✅ How to build a diabetes-friendly, high-protein meal plan 🍽️
✅ Common protein myths debunked – what’s REALLY good for diabetics?

🔥 Best Diabetes-Friendly Proteins in This Video:
🍳 Eggs – The ultimate blood sugar-stabilizing protein
🐟 Fatty Fish (Salmon, Sardines, Mackerel) – Omega-3 powerhouse for inflammation control
🥛 Greek Yogurt – Probiotic-rich protein for gut health & glucose stability
🍗 Chicken Breast – A lean, high-protein option for steady energy
🌱 Lentils & Tofu – The best plant-based proteins for insulin function
🧀 Cottage Cheese – A slow-digesting protein for long-lasting blood sugar balance

🚨 Worst Protein Choices for Blood Sugar:
❌ Processed meats – Full of sodium and hidden carbs
❌ Fried & breaded proteins – Cause glucose spikes and increase inflammation
❌ Flavored yogurt – Packed with added sugars that sabotage blood sugar control

📌 Want to Keep Your Blood Sugar Stable? Try This:
🔹 Choose lean proteins over processed meats 🥩
🔹 Pair protein with fiber to prevent glucose spikes 🌱
🔹 Eat high-protein meals to control cravings and maintain energy 🚀

💬 Which protein source do YOU eat the most? Comment below! 👇

title: Early Diabetes Signs  Don’t Mis |10 Early Symptoms of Diabetes
description:
Early Diabetes Signs  Don’t Mis |10 Early Symptoms of Diabetes 

Diabetes can manifest silently and unexpectedly, making early diagnosis essential to prevent complications. Understanding and recognizing subtle signs is crucial for timely intervention. In this video, we'll delve into key symptoms like frequent urination, constant thirst (polydipsia), extreme hunger (polyphagia), and persistent fatigue. Learn about the significance of blurred vision, delayed healing, tingling sensations, skin changes, and emotional fluctuations as potential indicators of diabetes. Don't ignore these warning signs; consult a healthcare professional for early detection and management. Empower yourself with knowledge and take proactive steps for a healthier future.  Diabetes Signs

title: Diabetes Symptoms 🚨 | Early Warning Signs & Blood Sugar Tests 🩸 #DiabetesAwareness @HealthIsBehind
description:
Do you know the early signs of diabetes? 🚨 Watch this video to learn about diabetes symptoms, blood sugar levels, and home diabetes tests 🩸. Stay informed & take action early! #DiabetesAwareness #BloodSugar #HealthTips #DiabetesPrevention #healthisbehind @HealthIsBehind 
🔴 10 Silent Symptoms of Diabetes You Shouldn’t Ignore! 🚨

1️⃣ Extreme hunger 🍽️
2️⃣ Unexplained weight loss ⚖️
3️⃣ Fatigue & weakness 😓
4️⃣ Frequent urination 🚽
5️⃣ Blurry vision 👀
6️⃣ Tingling in hands & feet 🤲
7️⃣ Slow-healing wounds 🩹
8️⃣ Dry & itchy skin 🦵
9️⃣ Increased thirst 🥤
🔟 Skin infections 🦠

✅ How to Check Blood Sugar at Home:
✔️ Glucometer 🩸 (Prick finger, insert strip, read result)
✔️ Urine Test 🚽 (Dip strip, compare color)
✔️ CGM 📟 (Sensor for continuous monitoring)

🔍 Early Detection = Better Health! If you notice these signs, consult a doctor. Stay aware, stay healthy! 💙

📌 Follow for more health tips! ✅ #DiabetesAwareness #BloodSugar #HealthTips

title: Diabetes and Pregnancy
Keto bread in 5 minutes - ideal for diabetes, weight loss and heart health! 💪🍞
description:
Keto bread in 5 minutes - ideal for diabetes, weight loss and heart health! 💪🍞

Hello, friends! Today we are making quick and healthy keto bread - ideal for healthy eating! And I will also show you how to make delicious sandwiches with it. Be sure to save the recipe and subscribe so you don't miss new healthy videos! 💚

Calories in the recipe:
Keto bread (for the whole volume): ~850 kcal.
1 serving of bread (1/6 of one loaf): ~70 kcal.
Sandwiches (for 1 serving): ~150 kcal.

Be sure to consult your doctor before use.
This keto bread is not only delicious, but can also be useful for a number of health problems:

1. Insulin resistance and diabetes 🩸🔬
It does not contain wheat flour and sugar, so it does not cause sharp jumps in blood sugar.
Flaxseed and sunflower seeds slow down the absorption of carbohydrates and improve insulin sensitivity.
2. Overweight and obesity ⚖️🔥
High content of protein and healthy fats gives a long feeling of satiety and reduces cravings for snacks.
Flaxseed and cottage cheese support metabolism and bowel function.
3. Cardiovascular diseases ❤️🫀
Omega-3 fatty acids (from flaxseed, fish and avocado) reduce the level of bad cholesterol, strengthen blood vessels and reduce the risk of heart attacks.
Olive oil contains antioxidants that protect the heart from inflammation.
4. Intestinal and digestive problems 🏃‍♂️🌿
Flaxseed and sunflower seeds are rich in fiber, which helps the intestines work and eliminates constipation.
Probiotics from cottage cheese support healthy intestinal microflora.
5. Decreased energy and fatigue ⚡💪
Healthy fats provide long-lasting energy without sugar surges.
Potassium from avocado maintains fluid balance and muscle function.
This bread is a great choice for a healthy lifestyle! 🥑🐟 Suitable for keto diets, diabetics and anyone who wants to feel better. 💚

Benefits of products:
✅ Flaxseed is a source of Omega-3, improves digestion and supports the heart.
✅ Cottage cheese – rich in protein and calcium, strengthens bones and muscles.
✅ Avocado – contains healthy fats and potassium, helps the heart.
✅ Red fish – rich in Omega-3, improves the condition of the skin and blood vessels.
✅ Olive oil – rich in antioxidants, good for the heart.

Cook this delicious keto bread and share your impressions in the comments! Don't forget to like and subscribe if you like healthy recipes. 😊✨

Recipe and ingredients:
250 g of fat cottage cheese.
1 egg.
125 g of flaxseed.
50 g of sunflower seeds.
a little salt.
1 teaspoon of baking powder.
sun-dried tomatoes.

I will take 2 4x6 inch molds.
drizzle with vegetable oil.
sprinkle with sesame seeds.
grease my hands with vegetable oil.
shape the bread.
a little more sesame seeds.
bake for 35-40 minutes at 180 °C.

For sandwiches:
1/2 avocado.
lightly salted red fish.
a little lettuce.
soft cheese.
a little lemon.
black pepper.
a little olive oil.
a little sesame seeds.
olive oil.

title: গাজর কীভাবে ডায়াবেটিস রোগীদের অন্ধত্ব আটকায় ? Diabetes control Tips in Bengali | Dr Biswas
description:
গাজর কীভাবে ডায়াবেটিস রোগীদের অন্ধত্ব আটকায় ?

অনেকে ভাবেন ডায়াবেটিস মানে শুধুই blood sugar level বাড়া | blood sugar বাড়া আসলে ডায়াবেটিস রোগের ট্রেইলার | আসল সিনেমা আরো অনেক বিপজ্জনক | ডায়াবেটিসের বিপজ্জনক সিনেমার একটি হলো অন্ধত্ব | হ্যাঁ ঠিকই শুনেছেন ডায়াবেটিস হলে আপনি অন্ধও হয়ে যেতে পারেন | অন্ধত্ব তো শেষ ধাপ, তার আগে চোখের আরো অনেকগুলি সমস্যায় আপনাকে ভুগতে হবে | ডায়াবেটিস রোগীদের চোখের এই রোগকে Diabetes retinopathy বলা হয় | গবেষণা থেকে দেখা যাচ্ছে ১০০ জন ডায়াবেটিস রোগীর মধ্যে ২৭ থেকে ৩৫ জনেরই Diabetes retinopathy তে ভোগেন | বুঝতেই পারছেন আপনারও ডায়াবেটিস রেটিনোপ্যাথি ও তার থেকে অন্ধত্বের সম্ভাবনা অনেকটাই বেশি | 

গাজর আপনাকে এবিষয়ে সাহায্য করতে পারে । তবে শুধু গাজরে এত বড় সমস্যা আটকানো সম্ভব না | সেগুলি নিয়েও আলোচনা করব,  আগে ডায়াবেটিস রোগীর অন্ধত্ব আটকাতে গাজরের ভূমিকা জেনে নিন | সাথে জেনে নিন কীভাবে গাজর খেলে ব্লাড সুগার বাড়তে পারে |

গাজরের ইংলিশ নাম তো জানেন - ‌Carrot | Carrot এ থাকে Carotenoid নামে একটি বিশেষ ফাইটোকেমিক্যাল | প্রায় ৬০০ রকমের ক্যারোটিনয়েড পাওয়া যায় | তার মধ্যে আপনি তো বিটা ক্যারোটিন ও আলফা ক্যারোটিনের তো নাম শুনেছেন | এই দুটিই আপনার শরীরে Vitamin A তে রূপান্তরিত হতে পারে | গাজরে দুটি ক্যারোটিনই বেশ ভালো পরিমানে পাওয়া যায়

২০১৫ এর একটি গবেষণা থেকে দেখা যাচ্ছে গাজরের মতো আলফা ক্যারোটিন ও বিটা ক্যারোটিন যুক্ত খাবার Type 2 Diabetes এর সম্ভাবনা কমাতে পারে ।

আবার গাজরের ক্যারোটিনয়েড চোখের রেটিনাতেও জমা হয় | ক্যারোটিনয়েড ক্ষতিকর নীল আলোকে শোষণ করে নেয়, ফলে রেটিনা ক্ষতিগ্রস্ত হয় না | ক্যারোটিনয়েড অন্যদিকে ক্ষতিকর ফ্রি রেডিক্যালজনিত অক্সিডেটিভ স্ট্রেস ও ইনফ্লামেশন কমিয়েও চোখকে রক্ষা করে | ফলে আপনি যদি নিয়মিত গাজর খান ডায়াবেটিস রেটিনোপ্যাথি হতেও বাঁধা পায় |

এখন প্রশ্ন হলো কীভাবে গাজরের ক্যারোটিনয়েডের কার্যকারিতা বাড়িয়ে ডায়াবেটিস রেটিনোপ্যাথি আটকাতে আরো বেশি লাভ পাবেন ?

গবেষণা থেকে দেখা যাচ্ছে কাঁচা গাজর থেকে সিদ্ধ গাজরের ক্যারোটিনয়েডর জৈব উপলভ্যতা অনেক বেশি | আবার গাজরের সাথে ফ্যাট মেশাতে পারলে আপনার শরীরে ক্যারোটিনয়েডর শোষণ বেড়ে যায় | মানে আপনি যদি গাজরকে সিদ্ধ করে বা রান্না করে ডিম, মাছ, বাদামের মতো  ফ্যাটসমৃদ্ধ খাবারের সাথে খেতে পারেন তাহলে ডায়াবেটিস রেটিনোপ্যাথি আটকানো আরো সহজ হবে |

তবে এখানে একটা কিন্তু আছে | গাজরকে সিদ্ধ করলে গাজরের গ্লাইসেমিক ইন্ডেক্স অনেক বেড়ে যায় , ফলে সুগার বাড়ার ভয় থাকে | তাহলে উপায় ? তারও উপায় আছে | পরের ভিডিওতে আমরা সেটাও জানব | তারজন্য বামদিকের ভিডিওটি দেখুন |


এটি একটি শিক্ষামূলক আলোচনা যা বিভিন্ন গবেষণাপত্রের তথ্য নির্ভর ।

Many believe honey is a natural and healthier alternative to sugar, but does it really work for diabetics? Honey still raises blood sugar levels, so it should be consumed in moderation and with caution! 

⚠️ Important Tips for Diabetics:
✔️ Prefer raw, unprocessed honey 🍯
✔️ Consume in small amounts & with fiber-rich foods
✔️ Monitor your blood sugar levels regularly 📉
✔️ Consult a doctor before making changes to your diet
